title: notelaea ligustrina
intro: Notelaea ligustrina, also known as black olive or black olive tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Aboriginal Australian medicine, where it is used to treat various health issues. The leaves and bark of Notelaea ligustrina contain compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are believed to provide health benefits such as reducing fever, soothing skin irritations, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, the leaves are often used to make teas, infusions, and poultices, while the bark is used to make a decoction that can be applied topically or ingested.
